mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series 418d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 418d engine typically costs £2,200–£6,500 supply only depending on generation. N47-era engines sit at the lower end (£2,200–£4,500), while B47 units run £2,800–£6,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Rebuilt N47D20C units for F32/F33 chassis are available with 12-month warranty options, saving up to 60% vs dealership. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 4 Series 418d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which of the two engine generations your 418d has. The N47 (2013–2015) has a well-documented timing chain weakness — the chain is mounted at the rear of the engine, making replacement an engine-out job. The B47 (2015+) is a significant improvement, but EGR cooler failure (subject to a major BMW recall) and DPF clogging remain common issues. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series 418d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain failure on the N47 (pre-2015), EGR cooler failure on both N47 and B47 (subject to a 1.6 million vehicle recall), and DPF clogging (particularly with urban driving). The N47 timing chain is the most critical — it can snap without warning, causing catastrophic engine damage.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series 418d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept £7,000 418d, a £4,000 recon is 57% of the car's value — borderline. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. For B47-engined cars (post-2015), higher vehicle values make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series 418d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: 2013–2015 is N47D20U1 (143 bhp), 2015–2020 is B47D20A (136–150 bhp). The 418d was fitted to F32 (Coupe), F33 (Convertible), and F36 (Gran Coupe) chassis. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£3,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap requiring a full engine replacement. It is recommended to replace the timing chain after 100,000 km, but symptoms may appear sooner. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Note that the N47's rear-mounted chain makes this an engine-out job.

eraMap.title: The 418d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Rear-mounted timing chain — the defining N47 weakness
Modular B47 — improved chain design, EGR/DPF remain issues

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current B47 generation, the most common quote request today. Older N47 generations: see the Era Map above for era-specific pricing, which runs lower. Rebuilt N47D20C units for F32/F33 chassis (2012–2020) are available with 12-month warranty options, saving up to 60% vs dealership.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 4 Series 418d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). N47 engines require additional labour due to the rear-mounted timing chain access — it's an engine-out job.

cta.label: → Compare 4 Series 418d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N47 Timing Chain Failure (Early 418d, 2013–2015)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, loss of power. The chain can snap without warning.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — failures have been reported from as little as 62,000 miles.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The N47's timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine, making access impossible without removing the engine entirely. The chain's role is to synchronise the pistons and valves — if it snaps, the inlet and exhaust valves collide with the pistons, resulting in massive engine damage including blown pistons, bent valves, and shattered rocker arms. It is recommended to replace the timing chain after 100,000 km, but symptoms may appear sooner. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (N47 and B47)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, engine warning light, in severe cases, smouldering particles and fire risk.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — BMW recalled 1.6 million diesel vehicles globally over EGR cooler fire risk.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignoring coolant loss can lead to overheating and engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the coolant warning comes on, pull over immediately.

problems.recommendation: Glycol leakage from the EGR cooler can occur, and in combination with soot deposits and high temperatures, this might result in smouldering particles. BMW's recall covers vehicles manufactured between May 2013 and July 2017. Check whether your 418d has had the recall work completed. Reconditioned engines for the 418d include updated EGR components designed to prevent the carbon buildup that specifically affects 4 Series 418d models.

problems.title: DPF Clogging (N47 and B47)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced performance, poor fuel economy, increased regeneration frequency, engine warning light.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — accelerated by short urban journeys.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignoring DPF issues can lead to turbocharger damage.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if DPF warning appears.

problems.recommendation: The 418d's DPF is sensitive to short journeys — the filter requires regular motorway runs to allow passive regeneration. If you primarily drive in town, the DPF will clog faster. A forced regeneration or DPF clean can restore function, but if ignored, the turbocharger can be damaged by excessive back pressure. Some owners report a high-speed noise above 60mph that may be linked to DPF or turbo issues.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Recall may cover it — check with BMW first
Forced regeneration or clean — do it when warning appears
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Viable if the rest of the vehicle is solid

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 418d? Check These First
buyIf: * 2015+ B47 with EGR recall completed — improved reliability over the N47
* N47 with documented timing chain replacement (especially over 60,000 miles)
* Full service history with oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — the rear chain is an engine-out job
* No proof of EGR recall completion on any 418d
* DPF warning light or evidence of DPF issues
* Poor service history — both N47 and B47 are sensitive to oil quality

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 418d is BMW's entry-level 4 Series diesel — it offers excellent fuel economy (up to 62 mpg) but comes with well-documented diesel engine vulnerabilities. The N47 (pre-2015) is the riskier buy — timing chain failure is common and expensive to fix. The B47 (2015+) is significantly improved, but EGR cooler and DPF issues remain. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £4,000 recon on a £6,000 418d is 67% — borderline. On a sub-£5,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare 4 Series 418d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 4 Series 418d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (N47 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47/B47) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Recall Check — Has the EGR cooler recall been completed? (1.6 million vehicles affected)
* DPF Status — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys accelerate clogging
* Smoke Check — White smoke could indicate EGR cooler coolant leak; black smoke could indicate DPF or injector issues
* Turbo Check — Test acceleration; any whistling, lag, or power loss?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
